content:
Introduction
The Rest Operator (...) and Spread Operator (...) are two powerful JavaScript features introduced in ES6. Although they use the same syntax, they serve completely different purposes. Understanding the difference between them is essential for writing clean, modern, and efficient JavaScript code.
What is the Rest Operator?
The Rest Operator collects multiple elements and packs them into a single array.
Syntax
function displaySkills(...skills) {
console.log(skills);
}displaySkills("JavaScript", "React", "Node.js");Output
["JavaScript", "React", "Node.js"]Common Use Cases

• Function parameters

• Destructuring arrays

• Handling variable number of arguments
Example: Array Destructuring
const [first, ...remaining] = [10, 20, 30, 40];console.log(first); // 10
console.log(remaining); // [20, 30, 40]What is the Spread Operator?
The Spread Operator expands elements from an array or object into individual values.
Syntax
const numbers = [1, 2, 3];console.log(...numbers);Output
1 2 3Common Use Cases

• Copying arrays

• Merging arrays

• Copying objects

• Passing array elements as function arguments
Example: Merging Arrays
const frontend = ["HTML", "CSS"];
const backend = ["Node.js", "Express"];const fullStack = [...frontend, ...backend];console.log(fullStack);Output
["HTML", "CSS", "Node.js", "Express"]Key Differences Between Rest and Spread Operators
difference b/w rest and spread operatorQuick Example
Rest Operator
function sum(...numbers) {
return numbers.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
}Here, multiple arguments are collected into a single array.
Spread Operator
const nums = [10, 20, 30];console.log(...nums);Here, array elements are expanded into individual values.

Easy Trick to Remember
Rest Operator
👉 “Collects multiple values into one.”
function demo(...args) {}Spread Operator
👉 “Spreads one collection into multiple values.”
const copy = [...array];Conclusion
Although the Rest and Spread Operators share the same ... syntax, they perform opposite tasks. The Rest Operator gathers multiple values into a single array, while the Spread Operator expands arrays or objects into individual elements. Mastering these operators will help you write cleaner, more concise, and modern JavaScript code, especially when working with React, Node.js, and ES6+ applications.
